info.title: Save Claim API
info.description:
Creates a **new claim** in the John Deere Warranty System using **work order data**. The integration can be configured to **auto-submit** the claim or **save as a draft** for manual submission through the **JDWS Portal**. Multiple legacy claim types are consolidated into this API — valid fields and values depend on the claim type; use your **Multiple Option Values**, **Save Claim Field Legend**, **SOAP-to-REST field mapping**, and **REST API FAQ** attachments.

**Note:** The request body must contain **at least one** valid field.

Support: Warranty_Api_Support@JohnDeere.com

servers.description: Sandbox (cert). Production uses `https://warrantyext-api-prod.deere.com` — confirm the path prefix under `/digcust/wy/` and whether the `cert` segment is present for your environment.

OAuth2.description: John Deere Dealer Solutions OAuth 2.0. Use the **authorization code** flow (three-legged) for interactive users, or the **client credentials** grant for machine-to-machine access. Request the OAuth scope assigned to your application for this API (this reference uses `warranty` as an example — confirm the scope in your Developer.Deere.com application and technical documentation).

SaveClaimRequest.description: Payload to create a **Claim** via **ClaimCollection**. Include **at least one** valid field for your claim type and program (see **Save Claim Field Legend** and SOAP-to-REST mapping). Additional properties are accepted per the underlying OData contract.

dealerAccount.description: Dealer account identifier when required for the claim key or header context.

segment.description: Segment code when required for the claim.

workOrder.description: Work order number driving the claim creation.

submitMode.description: When supported by your configuration, indicates auto-submit vs. draft behavior (exact values depend on your program — confirm in field legend).

SaveClaimResponse.description: Response after the claim is persisted. The service returns the **claim number** (claim no.) when creation succeeds. Additional fields may appear per claim type.

claimStatus.description: High-level status when returned (for example draft vs. submitted).

message.example: The request body must include at least one valid field.

post.description:
Creates a **normal claim** in the warranty database from work order data. The response includes the **claim number** when successful. The body must contain **at least one** valid field.

**Sandbox example:** `POST https://jdws-api-sandbox.deere.com/digcust/wy/cert/WtySaveClaim/ClaimCollection`

**Production:** Use the production host (`https://warrantyext-api-prod.deere.com`) with the path pattern documented for your program (the `cert` segment or application id may differ from sandbox).

**Accept:** `application/json`

parameters.name: applicationId
parameters.description: Application or service instance segment for your Dealer Solutions integration (sandbox example: `WtySaveClaim`).

200.description: Claim created; response includes the claim number and may include additional entity fields.

400.description: Bad request — empty body, no valid fields, or validation failure.

error.message: The request body must include at least one valid field.

401.description: Unauthorized. Missing or invalid access token.

403.description: Forbidden — token does not include the required scope or organization access.

error.message: Access token is missing the required scope.
An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.
